wikitext.*:
[[File:Micropetplayland.jpg|thumb]]
By clicking on the Bag Button<ref>[[File:ShopViewCircle.jpg|thumb|left|The bag is the fifth icon on the bottom bar of the game.]]{{Clear}}</ref>and then on the Micropets button,<ref>[[File:BirbsInventory.jpg|thumb|left|Click the Micropets button to get to the Micropet Playland.]]{{Clear}}</ref> you will get to the [[Micropet Playland]]. You will see a grassy area with your birb and ten of your [[micropets]] in it. They each move around in their own way, such as walking, hopping, flying, etc. Some will peek out from behind bushes. You can click on a micropet to hear its' unique sound. 

The grid under the playland shows all of your micropets in green squares, as well as the micropet egg you're currently hatching. If you don't have an egg, you can get one by clicking the small head in the top right corner marked "Lab." This is [[Professor Oat's Lab]], where [[Professor Oat]] will offer you an egg and make you link a goal of your choice to it. The egg hatches a new micropet baby after finishing the goal seven times. Afterwards, you will be able to grow the baby into an adult by taking it on  fifteen adventures.

[[File:Micropetindex.jpg|thumb]]You can change the grid to a bulleted list, which shows all of your micropets, with their names, state of growth (baby or adult), and how many adventures they have been on with you.  

In either display format, click the micropet image to see it in more detail. This will show its' name, stage of growth (baby or adult), pronouns, the number of adventures it has been on, its' nature, and a description. There is a [[Micropets#Settings|settings]] wheel in the upper right which allows you to change its' name, pronouns, and toggle its' ability to grow.

Use the Equip button to take it on an adventure with you. It takes 15 adventure days to grow a micropet to an adult, unless you flip the toggle to let it stay a baby forever. Its' animation here is different if it is equipped or not. 

==Micropedia==
[[File:Micropediaexample.jpg|thumb]]When clicking on the animal paw with the magnifying glass you can see a picture grid of the micropet types that you own. Click to see which of the three color variants you have, and how many of each. You can also see if you are missing some, as the index shows a question mark instead of a missing micropet. {{Clear}}
